message.content: read plan.md, you can ask me questions about the project to complete the design plan.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- commit-optimizer: Create structured optimization commit messages with measured performance/size results. Use this whenever you're committing code optimizations (especially RISC-V libm assembly tuning) and want to automatically benchmark the changes and generate a properly formatted commit message with problem, approach, and result sections. Invoke with `/commit`.
- libm-testpoints: IEEE 754 numerical analysis expert for generating representative libm test points. Use this skill whenever the user asks to generate test points, test cases, or test inputs for math library (libm) functions (sin, cos, exp, log, sqrt, pow, etc.), whether float or double precision. Also trigger when the user wants to improve, audit, or extend existing test_*.c files in the RISC-V libm test harness. Trigger on requests like "generate 100 test points for sinf", "what are the key test cases for expf?", or "add representative tests to test_logf.c". The argument is the function name, e.g. "/libm-testpoints sinf" or "/libm-testpoints sin".
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- statusline: Set up Claude Code's status line UI
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
- insights: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions
- team-onboarding: Help teammates ramp on Claude Code with a guide from your usage

content.content:
1	# Implementation Plan: Autonomous Agentic libnn Optimization Workflow
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	libnn functions have multi-variant C implementations (plain C / DSP / RVV). Optimizing a function means writing a better RVV implementation for a specific FPGA target. Currently this is manual: read source, write code, build, test on QEMU, profile on FPGA, iterate.
6	
7	This plan defines how **Claude Code directly drives the optimization loop** -- no Python orchestration framework. The LLM reads source files, parses hardware configs, writes optimized C code, and calls existing shell scripts for build/test/profile. For state persistence across sessions, a simple JSON file tracks progress.
8	
9	The optimization is specialized for a **designated target configuration** (CPU, VLEN, cache hierarchy, ISA extensions) and **known static-time input shapes** (batch size, spatial dimensions, channel counts, kernel sizes, etc.) extracted from existing test/example C files. Dynamic shapes are not supported -- each optimization run targets a fixed set of input dimensions, enabling aggressive tiling, unrolling, and register allocation decisions that would not be valid for arbitrary shapes.
10	
11	**The baseline source code is never modified.** Instead, the agent creates a new C file (e.g., `opt/gen/riscv_nn_conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any.c`) that defines the same function symbol as the original. The build system substitutes the optimized `.o` for the original at link time. No macros, no `#ifdef` guards, no changes to the original source. A/B comparison is simply linking one `.o` or the other.
12	
13	The FPGA configuration (Verilog `.inc` file like `ax45mpv_v1024_8mb_config.inc`) provides the ground truth for target features: VLEN, DLEN, L2 cache size, ISA extensions, etc. Cache sizes are especially important since the library's tiling GEMM currently hardcodes L1=512KB -- the actual L2=8MB and L1 D-cache=32KB from the config should drive tiling parameter selection.
14	
15	## Two Modes of Operation
16	
17	### Mode 1: Single Function
18	
19	**Input:** A performance test file from `Examples/unit_performance/`, e.g.:
20	```
21	Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any.c
22	```
23	
24	The LLM reads this file and extracts:
25	- **Function name** from the `riscv_nn_*()` call site
26	- **All static input shapes** from the `#define` macros at the top of the file (`INPUT_X=16`, `INPUT_Y=15`, `INPUT_CH=4`, `KERNEL_X=2`, `CH_MULT=192`, `STRIDE_X=1`, `PAD_X=0`, etc.)
27	- **Quantization parameters** (`OUT_SHIFT`, `OUT_MULT`, `INPUT_OFFSET`, `FILTER_OFFSET`, `OUTPUT_OFFSET`, activation min/max)
28	- **Data types** inferred from the function name suffix (`u8_u8_u8`, `s8_s8_s8`, etc.)
29	
30	No separate TOML or config file needed -- the perf test file *is* the task specification.
31	
32	### Mode 2: Complete Model
33	
34	**Input:** A model example C file, e.g.:
35	```
36	Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/nds_nn_examples_cifar10.c
37	```
38	
39	The LLM reads the model file and its companion parameter header (found via `#include`) to extract the full op sequence with concrete shapes:
40	
41	| # | Function | Key shapes |
42	|---|----------|-----------|
43	| 1 | `conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_RGB_sft_bias_fast` | im_dim=32, im_ch=3, ker=5, out_ch=32, stride=1, pad=2 |
44	| 2 | `relu_s8` | size=32*32*32 |
45	| 3 | `maxpool_HWC_s8` | im_dim=32, ch=32, ker=3, stride=2 |
46	| 4 | `conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` | im_dim=16, im_ch=32, ker=5, out_ch=16 |
47	| 5 | `relu_s8` | size=16*16*16 |
48	| 6 | `maxpool_HWC_s8` | im_dim=16, ch=16, ker=3, stride=2 |
49	| 7 | `conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` | im_dim=8, im_ch=16, ker=5, out_ch=32 |
50	| 8 | `relu_s8` | size=8*8*32 |
51	| 9 | `maxpool_HWC_s8` | im_dim=8, ch=32, ker=3, stride=2 |
52	| 10 | `fc_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` | in_dim=512, out=10 |
53	| 11 | `softmax_s8_fast` | size=10 |
54	
55	The LLM profiles all (function, shape-set) pairs and optimizes every one of them to reach the best total model cycle count. Cost per pair is measured directly -- baseline cycles at that exact shape, multiplied by how many times the model invokes the function with that shape. Same function with two different shape sets = two independent pairs with independent costs (a 32x32 conv and an 8x8 conv share a name but not a cycle budget). Ranking by per-pair total cycles only sets work *order* so the largest wins land first and model-level cycles drop monotonically; it does not gate coverage. The long tail is still specialized -- a small kernel invoked many times can dominate once the heavy convs are tuned.
56	
57	## Directory Structure
58	
59	```
60	agentic_libnn_opt/
61	  plan.md                                   # This file
62	  opt/
63	    config.toml                             # Target config (toolchain, FPGA, QEMU paths)
64	    state.json                              # Optimization state (persists across sessions)
65	    gen/                                    # Generated optimized source files
66	      riscv_nn_<func>.c                     # One per optimized function
67	    log/                                    # Build/test/perf logs per iteration
68	  ax45mpv_v1024_8mb_config.inc              # FPGA Verilog config (input)
69	```
70	
71	## Configuration
72	
73	Single TOML file at `opt/config.toml` -- read directly by the LLM, no schema validation layer needed:
74	
75	```toml
76	[toolchain]
77	bin_dir = "/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin"
78	prefix = "riscv64-elf-"
79	
80	[target]
81	cpu = "AX45MPV"
82	fpga_config = "ax45mpv_v1024_8mb_config.inc"
83	
84	[qemu]
85	binary = "/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64"
86	vlen = 1024
87	timeout = 120
88	
89	[fpga]
90	host = "sw-boards.andestech.com"
91	port = 1116
92	timeout = 300
93	
94	[limits]
95	max_iterations = 10
96	min_improvement_pct = 2.0
97	```
98	
99	## State File
100	
101	`opt/state.json` -- simple JSON the LLM reads/writes to track progress across sessions:
102	
103	```json
104	{
105	  "mode": "single_function",
106	  "function": "conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any",
107	  "source_input": "Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any.c",
108	  "phase": "evaluate",
109	  "iteration": 3,
110	  "baseline": { "cycles": 45200, "instructions": 32100 },
111	  "best": { "cycles": 38900, "instructions": 28500, "iteration": 2, "commit": "e4f5a6b" },
112	  "history": [
113	    { "iteration": 1, "commit": "a1b2c3d", "cycles": 42000, "instructions": 30200, "accepted": true,  "notes": "unrolled inner loop 4x" },
114	    { "iteration": 2, "commit": "e4f5a6b", "cycles": 38900, "instructions": 28500, "accepted": true,  "notes": "tiled for L1 dcache 32KB" },
115	    { "iteration": 3, "commit": "9c8d7e6", "cycles": 39100, "instructions": 27800, "accepted": false, "notes": "register pressure regression" }
116	  ]
117	}
118	```
119	
120	Field semantics:
121	- `mode`: `"single_function"` or `"model"`. Drives which loop variant runs.
122	- `function`: bare function name (no `riscv_nn_` prefix). Keys the `opt/gen/` source path.
123	- `source_input`: path to the perf test `.c` (single mode) or model `.c` (model mode). The task spec.
124	- `phase`: current loop phase (`gather`, `baseline`, `iterate`, `evaluate`, `done`). Resumed on session restart.
125	- `iteration`: count of iterations attempted so far (accepted + rejected).
126	- `baseline.cycles` / `baseline.instructions`: unmodified library measurement. Reference point for speedup.
127	- `best.cycles` / `best.instructions`: measurements from the winning iteration.
128	- `best.iteration`: which `history` entry is the winner.
129	- `best.commit`: SHA of the commit holding the winning `opt/gen/<func>.c`. Required -- if the `.o` cache is evicted, check this SHA out and rebuild to regenerate the exact artifact. Also the anchor for `git revert` on reject.
130	- `history[i].iteration`: monotonic integer, one per attempt.
131	- `history[i].commit`: SHA of that attempt's commit. Required for the same reason as `best.commit`.
132	- `history[i].cycles` / `instructions`: measured counts. Comparable only within the same (function, shape-set, target).
133	- `history[i].accepted`: `true` only if accuracy passed AND `cycles < best.cycles` at that moment. Rejected entries stay so future sessions don't retry the same idea.
134	- `history[i].notes`: one-line description of the idea.
135	
136	Best selection: `best = argmin(cycles)` over `history` entries with `accepted=true`; `best.commit` points at the source to restore.
137	
138	## Optimization Loop
139	
140	The LLM executes this loop directly, calling shell scripts at each mechanical step:
141	
142	### Phase 1

file.content:
# Implementation Plan: Autonomous Agentic libnn Optimization Workflow

## Context

libnn functions have multi-variant C implementations (plain C / DSP / RVV). Optimizing a function means writing a better RVV implementation for a specific FPGA target. Currently this is manual: read source, write code, build, test on QEMU, profile on FPGA, iterate.

This plan defines how **Claude Code directly drives the optimization loop** -- no Python orchestration framework. The LLM reads source files, parses hardware configs, writes optimized C code, and calls existing shell scripts for build/test/profile. For state persistence across sessions, a simple JSON file tracks progress.

The optimization is specialized for a **designated target configuration** (CPU, VLEN, cache hierarchy, ISA extensions) and **known static-time input shapes** (batch size, spatial dimensions, channel counts, kernel sizes, etc.) extracted from existing test/example C files. Dynamic shapes are not supported -- each optimization run targets a fixed set of input dimensions, enabling aggressive tiling, unrolling, and register allocation decisions that would not be valid for arbitrary shapes.

**The baseline source code is never modified.** Instead, the agent creates a new C file (e.g., `opt/gen/riscv_nn_conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any.c`) that defines the same function symbol as the original. The build system substitutes the optimized `.o` for the original at link time. No macros, no `#ifdef` guards, no changes to the original source. A/B comparison is simply linking one `.o` or the other.

The FPGA configuration (Verilog `.inc` file like `ax45mpv_v1024_8mb_config.inc`) provides the ground truth for target features: VLEN, DLEN, L2 cache size, ISA extensions, etc. Cache sizes are especially important since the library's tiling GEMM currently hardcodes L1=512KB -- the actual L2=8MB and L1 D-cache=32KB from the config should drive tiling parameter selection.

## Two Modes of Operation

### Mode 1: Single Function

**Input:** A performance test file from `Examples/unit_performance/`, e.g.:
```
Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any.c
```

The LLM reads this file and extracts:
- **Function name** from the `riscv_nn_*()` call site
- **All static input shapes** from the `#define` macros at the top of the file (`INPUT_X=16`, `INPUT_Y=15`, `INPUT_CH=4`, `KERNEL_X=2`, `CH_MULT=192`, `STRIDE_X=1`, `PAD_X=0`, etc.)
- **Quantization parameters** (`OUT_SHIFT`, `OUT_MULT`, `INPUT_OFFSET`, `FILTER_OFFSET`, `OUTPUT_OFFSET`, activation min/max)
- **Data types** inferred from the function name suffix (`u8_u8_u8`, `s8_s8_s8`, etc.)

No separate TOML or config file needed -- the perf test file *is* the task specification.

### Mode 2: Complete Model

**Input:** A model example C file, e.g.:
```
Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/nds_nn_examples_cifar10.c
```

The LLM reads the model file and its companion parameter header (found via `#include`) to extract the full op sequence with concrete shapes:

| # | Function | Key shapes |
|---|----------|-----------|
| 1 | `conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_RGB_sft_bias_fast` | im_dim=32, im_ch=3, ker=5, out_ch=32, stride=1, pad=2 |
| 2 | `relu_s8` | size=32*32*32 |
| 3 | `maxpool_HWC_s8` | im_dim=32, ch=32, ker=3, stride=2 |
| 4 | `conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` | im_dim=16, im_ch=32, ker=5, out_ch=16 |
| 5 | `relu_s8` | size=16*16*16 |
| 6 | `maxpool_HWC_s8` | im_dim=16, ch=16, ker=3, stride=2 |
| 7 | `conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` | im_dim=8, im_ch=16, ker=5, out_ch=32 |
| 8 | `relu_s8` | size=8*8*32 |
| 9 | `maxpool_HWC_s8` | im_dim=8, ch=32, ker=3, stride=2 |
| 10 | `fc_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` | in_dim=512, out=10 |
| 11 | `softmax_s8_fast` | size=10 |

The LLM profiles all (function, shape-set) pairs and optimizes every one of them to reach the best total model cycle count. Cost per pair is measured directly -- baseline cycles at that exact shape, multiplied by how many times the model invokes the function with that shape. Same function with two different shape sets = two independent pairs with independent costs (a 32x32 conv and an 8x8 conv share a name but not a cycle budget). Ranking by per-pair total cycles only sets work *order* so the largest wins land first and model-level cycles drop monotonically; it does not gate coverage. The long tail is still specialized -- a small kernel invoked many times can dominate once the heavy convs are tuned.

## Directory Structure

```
agentic_libnn_opt/
  plan.md                                   # This file
  opt/
    config.toml                             # Target config (toolchain, FPGA, QEMU paths)
    state.json                              # Optimization state (persists across sessions)
    gen/                                    # Generated optimized source files
      riscv_nn_<func>.c                     # One per optimized function
    log/                                    # Build/test/perf logs per iteration
  ax45mpv_v1024_8mb_config.inc              # FPGA Verilog config (input)
```

## Configuration

Single TOML file at `opt/config.toml` -- read directly by the LLM, no schema validation layer needed:

```toml
[toolchain]
bin_dir = "/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin"
prefix = "riscv64-elf-"

[target]
cpu = "AX45MPV"
fpga_config = "ax45mpv_v1024_8mb_config.inc"

[qemu]
binary = "/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64"
vlen = 1024
timeout = 120

[fpga]
host = "sw-boards.andestech.com"
port = 1116
timeout = 300

[limits]
max_iterations = 10
min_improvement_pct = 2.0
```

## State File

`opt/state.json` -- simple JSON the LLM reads/writes to track progress across sessions:

```json
{
  "mode": "single_function",
  "function": "conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any",
  "source_input": "Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any.c",
  "phase": "evaluate",
  "iteration": 3,
  "baseline": { "cycles": 45200, "instructions": 32100 },
  "best": { "cycles": 38900, "instructions": 28500, "iteration": 2, "commit": "e4f5a6b" },
  "history": [
    { "iteration": 1, "commit": "a1b2c3d", "cycles": 42000, "instructions": 30200, "accepted": true,  "notes": "unrolled inner loop 4x" },
    { "iteration": 2, "commit": "e4f5a6b", "cycles": 38900, "instructions": 28500, "accepted": true,  "notes": "tiled for L1 dcache 32KB" },
    { "iteration": 3, "commit": "9c8d7e6", "cycles": 39100, "instructions": 27800, "accepted": false, "notes": "register pressure regression" }
  ]
}
```

Field semantics:
- `mode`: `"single_function"` or `"model"`. Drives which loop variant runs.
- `function`: bare function name (no `riscv_nn_` prefix). Keys the `opt/gen/` source path.
- `source_input`: path to the perf test `.c` (single mode) or model `.c` (model mode). The task spec.
- `phase`: current loop phase (`gather`, `baseline`, `iterate`, `evaluate`, `done`). Resumed on session restart.
- `iteration`: count of iterations attempted so far (accepted + rejected).
- `baseline.cycles` / `baseline.instructions`: unmodified library measurement. Reference point for speedup.
- `best.cycles` / `best.instructions`: measurements from the winning iteration.
- `best.iteration`: which `history` entry is the winner.
- `best.commit`: SHA of the commit holding the winning `opt/gen/<func>.c`. Required -- if the `.o` cache is evicted, check this SHA out and rebuild to regenerate the exact artifact. Also the anchor for `git revert` on reject.
- `history[i].iteration`: monotonic integer, one per attempt.
- `history[i].commit`: SHA of that attempt's commit. Required for the same reason as `best.commit`.
- `history[i].cycles` / `instructions`: measured counts. Comparable only within the same (function, shape-set, target).
- `history[i].accepted`: `true` only if accuracy passed AND `cycles < best.cycles` at that moment. Rejected entries stay so future sessions don't retry the same idea.
- `history[i].notes`: one-line description of the idea.

Best selection: `best = argmin(cycles)` over `history` entries with `accepted=true`; `best.commit` points at the source to restore.

## Optimization Loop

The LLM executes this loop directly, calling shell scripts at each mechanical step:

### Phase 1

2

6	
7	## Two Optimization Modes

file.content:
# Prompt: Autonomous Agentic libnn Optimization Workflow

## Goal

Design and implement an autonomous agentic optimization workflow for libnn.

## Two Optimization Modes

1. **Single function** - Optimize a single libnn function with specified input shapes.
2. **Model-level** - Optimize a set of libnn functions called by a given AI model, each with fixed input shapes.

## Target Hardware

- Optimization targets a specified FPGA configuration file (Verilog `.inc` format, e.g., `ax45mpv_v1024_8mb_config.inc`).
- Extract target features (VLEN, cache sizes, ISA extensions, etc.) from the FPGA config.

## User Configuration File

Allow the user to specify:
- Toolchain path
- Target architecture
- QEMU path
- FPGA address (domain:port)

## Deliverables

- `roadmap.md` - High-level vision document.
- `plan.md` - Step-by-step implementation plan for Claude Code, clear for both human and LLM readers.

## Constraints

- Each step contains exactly one feature.
- Each step has verifiable test cases.
- Each step includes: feature name, description, key files to modify, and dependencies on other steps.

### Phase 1

content.text: plan.md is truncated at Phase 1. Let me explore the codebase to understand the scripts and existing structure before asking questions.

input.prompt:
Explore the repo at /home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt (a libnn checkout) to understand the existing optimization infrastructure referenced by the plan.md file in that directory. Specifically:

1. Look at `qemu-validate/` scripts: build_libnn.sh, run_qemu_test.sh, run_all_tests.sh - what do they do, what args, what output format?
2. Look at `test_perf.sh` at repo root - what args (single function mode vs full), how is output structured (performance.csv)?
3. Look at `project/ax45mpv/` - board scripts used for FPGA profiling.
4. Look at `Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any.c` - confirm what macros/shape info can be extracted.
5. Look at `ax45mpv_v1024_8mb_config.inc` at repo root - what features does it contain (VLEN, cache sizes, ISA extensions)?
6. Look at `build.sh` - how the library is built; does `libnn.a` get produced at repo root?
7. Examine how a .c in Source/ with the same function symbol would override the original - is there a substitution mechanism, or would one just drop a .c next to it and rebuild?
8. Look at an Example model file like `Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/nds_nn_examples_cifar10.c` to see the op-sequence format.

Report concisely (<400 words) with file paths and short snippets of the key interfaces. This is for designing an autonomous agentic optimization loop - I need to know what scripts exist so I can wire them into Phase 2+ of the plan.

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a concise report:

## Summary

Based on exploration of `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt`, here are the key optimization infrastructure components:

### 1. QEMU Validation Scripts (`qemu-validate/`)

**`build_libnn.sh`** (lines 1-34)
- Builds libnn for AX45MPV with RVV (VLEN=1024)
- Output: `libnn.a` and `libnn.objdump` at repo root
- Calls: `/usr/bin/bash build.sh AX45MPV`

**`run_qemu_test.sh`** (lines 1-146)
- **Args**: `<test_name> [--strace] [--vlen N] [--timeout N]`
- **Example**: `run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --strace --vlen 1024`
- **Process**: Compiles test from `Examples/unit_func/t_${TEST_NAME}.c`, links with `libnn.a`, runs on QEMU
- **Output**: Parses strace for semihosting fd 9 writes; returns "PASS", "FAIL", "TIMEOUT", or "UNKNOWN"
- **Return codes**: 0=PASS, 1=FAIL, other=TIMEOUT/unknown

**`run_all_tests.sh`** (lines 1-142)
- Parallel test harness (parallelism and VLEN configurable)
- **Args**: `[PARALLEL] [VLEN]` (e.g., `16 1024`)
- **Output format**: Creates `results/` with per-test `.result` and `.log` files
- **Summary**: Prints PASS/FAIL/UNKNOWN counts; exits 0 on all-pass, 1 on fails, 2 on unknown

### 2. Performance Testing (`test_perf.sh` at repo root, lines 1-323)

**Single-function mode**:
- **Args**: `./test_perf.sh "" "" "" <FUNC_NAME>` (e.g., `avepool_HWC_s8`)
- Builds `t_<FUNC_NAME>.adx` from `Examples/unit_func/t_<FUNC_NAME>.c`
- **Build flags**: `-DENA_MEASURE_PERF=1 -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS -DPF_COUNTER`
- Runs via `SIM_WRAPPER` (board or QEMU)
- **Output parsing** (lines 299-303): Extracts `inst` and `cycle` with regex `grep -w 'inst|cycle' ${TEST_LOG}`
- **Output format**: "Result: <FUNC_NAME> inst=<N> cycle=<N>"

**Full mode**: Invokes `test.sh` to run complete test suite

### 3. FPGA Board Scripts (`project/ax45mpv/`)

**`run.sh`** (lines 1-313)
- Configuration: build flags, QEMU/board selection, vector ISA options
- **Key lines 192, 200-201**: Selects wrapper `riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board` for FPGA
- Compiles unit tests with `DPF_COUNTER` and `FUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE` macros
- Invokes `Examples/unit_func/run.sh` for individual function tests

### 4. Hardware Config (`ax45mpv_v1024_8mb_config.inc`)

**Verilog macro file** with target specifications:
- **VLEN=1024** (line 137: `define NDS_VLEN 1024`)
- **L2 cache**: 8MB (lines 60: `NDS_L2C_CACHE_SIZE_KB 8192`)
- **L1 D-cache**: 32KB, 4-way (line 326: `NDS_DCACHE_SIZE_KB 32`)
- **ISA extensions**: RVV (line 135: `RVV_SUPPORT "yes"`), VDOT support (line 145), FP16 (line 113)
- **ELEN=64, DLEN=1024** (lines 138-140)

### 5. Unit Performance Test Format (`Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any.c`)

**Static macros extracted**:
- Input shape: `INPUT_X=16, INPUT_Y=15, INPUT_CH=4`
- Kernel: `KERNEL_X=2, KERNEL_Y=3, CH_MULT=192`
- Padding/stride: `PAD_X=0, STRIDE_X=1`
- Quantization: `INPUT_OFFSET=-99, OUTPUT_OFFSET=89, OUT_SHIFT=-2, OUT_MULT`
- Activation bounds: `OUTPUT_ACTIVATION_MIN/MAX`
- Arrays: `uint8_t src1[SRC1_LEN], src2[SRC2_LEN], output[OUT_LEN]`
- **Function call** (line 72): `riscv_nn_conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any(...)`

### 6. Build System (`build.sh` and `Makefile_lib.mak`)

**`build.sh`** (lines 94-96, 248):
- **Invocation**: `./build.sh AX45MPV [extra_flags] [compiler] [output_dir]`
- **Produces**: `libnn.a` at repo root (or `${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/libnn.a`)
- **Flags**: Enables `-mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -fno-tree-slp-vectorize`

**`Makefile_lib.mak`** (lines 72-73):
- Compiles each `.c` in `Source/` subdirs to `.o`
- **Substitution mechanism**: VPATH includes `Source/ConvolutionFunctions/` etc.
- If `opt/gen/riscv_nn_conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any.c` exists in search path and compiles to `.o`, it shadows the original
- **Archive**: `ar crD libnn.a $(OBJS)` includes all `.o` files; **linker resolves symbol conflict as first match**

### 7. Model Example (`Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/nds_nn_examples_cifar10.c`)

**Op-sequence pattern** (lines 1-100):
- Includes parameter and weight headers: `nds_nn_examples_cifar10_parameter.h`, `nds_nn_examples_cifar10_weights.h`
- Function calls in sequence (implied by code structure): `conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_RGB_sft_bias_fast`, `relu_s8`, `maxpool_HWC_s8`, etc.
- Static buffer allocation: `uint8_t image_data[CONV1_IM_CH * CONV1_IM_DIM * CONV1_IM_DIM]`, `q7_t output_data[IP1_OUT]`

### Integration with Agent

**For Phase 2+ autonomy:**

1. **Source override**: Write optimized `.c` to `opt/gen/` with same function symbol; rebuild with modified VPATH to prioritize `opt/gen/`
2. **Profiling**: Call `test_perf.sh "" "" "" <FUNC>` and parse output for cycle/instruction counts
3. **Validation**: Call `run_qemu_test.sh <FUNC> --strace --vlen 1024` to verify correctness
4. **Hardware config**: Parse `ax45mpv_v1024_8mb_config.inc` for VLEN, cache sizes, ISA features
5. **Model analysis**: Extract function+shape pairs from model `.c` files via `#include` header chain; compute per-pair cost as (cycles_per_invocation Ă— invocation_count)

**Build command for override**: `make -f Makefile_lib.mak VPATH="opt/gen:Source/..." CFLAGS="..." CC=...` with `opt/gen` first in search path.

toolUseResult.prompt:
Explore the repo at /home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt (a libnn checkout) to understand the existing optimization infrastructure referenced by the plan.md file in that directory. Specifically:

1. Look at `qemu-validate/` scripts: build_libnn.sh, run_qemu_test.sh, run_all_tests.sh - what do they do, what args, what output format?
2. Look at `test_perf.sh` at repo root - what args (single function mode vs full), how is output structured (performance.csv)?
3. Look at `project/ax45mpv/` - board scripts used for FPGA profiling.
4. Look at `Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any.c` - confirm what macros/shape info can be extracted.
5. Look at `ax45mpv_v1024_8mb_config.inc` at repo root - what features does it contain (VLEN, cache sizes, ISA extensions)?
6. Look at `build.sh` - how the library is built; does `libnn.a` get produced at repo root?
7. Examine how a .c in Source/ with the same function symbol would override the original - is there a substitution mechanism, or would one just drop a .c next to it and rebuild?
8. Look at an Example model file like `Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/nds_nn_examples_cifar10.c` to see the op-sequence format.

Report concisely (<400 words) with file paths and short snippets of the key interfaces. This is for designing an autonomous agentic optimization loop - I need to know what scripts exist so I can wire them into Phase 2+ of the plan.

**`run_all_tests.sh`** (lines 1-142)

- **Args**: `./test_perf.sh "" "" "" <FUNC_NAME>` (e.g., `avepool_HWC_s8`)

- Runs via `SIM_WRAPPER` (board or QEMU)

Loaded the ToolSearch tools I need.

questions.question: How should the optimized .c file in opt/gen/ actually override the baseline symbol at link time?

options.label: Separate opt archive linked first
options.description: Build opt/gen/*.c into libnn_opt.a; link order `-lnn_opt -lnn` so the opt symbol wins. Baseline libnn.a untouched.

options.label: Rebuild libnn.a with VPATH priority
options.description: Add opt/gen to VPATH ahead of Source/; Makefile finds the opt .c first, original source is ignored. One archive.

options.label: Rename baseline .o before archiving
options.description: Build baseline, then swap in opt .o via `ar r libnn.a opt_gen.o` (replaces existing object).

questions.question: What is the primary ground-truth for per-iteration perf measurement during the loop?

options.label: FPGA via test_perf.sh BOARD
options.description: Every iteration profiles on FPGA (sw-boards.andestech.com:1116). Slow but ground truth. QEMU only for accuracy.

options.label: QEMU first, FPGA to confirm best
options.description: Iterate with QEMU cycle estimates; only run FPGA on the final winning candidate(s). Faster loop.

options.label: FPGA only, batch multiple candidates
options.description: Generate N candidates per iteration, profile them all together on FPGA to amortize board setup cost.

questions.question: How should iteration state be tied to git history?

options.label: Commit every attempt on an opt branch
options.description: Each iteration = one commit on branch `opt/<func>`. Rejects stay in history with accepted=false. `best.commit` points to winner.

options.label: Commit only accepted attempts
options.description: Rejects never hit git - the agent discards the .c and keeps a note in state.json. Only improvements become commits.

options.label: No auto-commit, state.json only
options.description: Agent never calls git. state.json carries full source of each attempt inline. User commits manually when satisfied.

questions.question: For Mode 2 (model), how does the agent extract the op sequence + per-op shapes?

options.label: LLM reads model.c + param header directly
options.description: Agent opens the .c and each #include'd parameter header, reads the riscv_nn_* call sites and macros, builds the table itself. No new parsing code.

options.label: Instrument build with a call tracer
options.description: Compile the model with a wrapper that logs every riscv_nn_* call + shape args at runtime, then parse the log.

options.label: Static parser script
options.description: Write a Python/shell helper that greps call sites and resolves macro values, emitting a JSON schedule consumed by the agent.
